item_spec.concrete_change:
Calibrate the design_review step's input effort by giving it a bounded reading budget and a prioritized reading list, instead of the open-ended 'read the project source' instruction that is unbounded on large repos (meta-audit-log O5: 511 files / 41k LOC with no budget). DISCOVERY TARGET — locate the design_review step-prompt rendering (likely in packages/audit-code/src/orchestrator, an executor, or a prompt template) in the implement phase. Changes:

(1) When rendering the design_review step, derive a TOP-N prioritized reading list from the existing risk_register.json (and/or coverage/unit manifests already loaded for other steps) — the highest-risk units first — and inline it as the suggested focus. The risk table is already surfaced to this step; turn it into an explicit ordered 'read these first' list rather than leaving prioritization implicit.

(2) State an explicit, bounded budget in the prompt: e.g., 'Focus on the top-N highest-risk units below; you do not need to read the entire repository. Reading roughly N units / a bounded set of files is sufficient to assess design.' Replace/augment the unbounded 'read the project source' wording so the bound is unambiguous while still allowing the host to read more if a thread demands it.

(3) Make N configurable (e.g. sessionConfig.design_review?.max_units, with a sensible default that scales down large repos) so the budget is tunable. Keep the step's OUTPUT obligation unchanged — this only bounds the input effort.

Edit the prompt SOURCE (rendered by `ensure`/the orchestrator), not generated output. CROSS-REFERENCE: add the design_review.max_units field to SessionConfig (shared/src/types/sessionConfig.ts; coordinate with the other SessionConfig additions in FINDING-005/008/009/010).

tests_to_write.name: design_review prompt includes a bounded, risk-ordered reading list
assertions: Given a risk_register fixture with several units, the rendered design_review prompt lists the top-N units in descending risk order.
The list length is capped at the configured budget N (a fixture with more than N units yields exactly N listed).
The prompt contains an explicit bound statement (focus on top-N / need not read the entire repo), not solely an open-ended 'read the project source' instruction.

tests_to_write.name: design_review reading budget is configurable with a default
assertions: An explicit sessionConfig.design_review.max_units is honored in the rendered list length.
With no config, a sensible default budget applies and does not exceed it for a large risk register.
